articles.title: Cryptos breakout of bear trends, but will it continue? Bitcoin (BTC) & Ethereum (ETH) Outlook
articles.expand: Crypto update: The crypto market extends its recent lead as Bitcoin climbs toward the $79,000 mark and Ethereum shatters major resistance levels. As traders navigate Kevin Warsh's hawkish Federal Reserve outlook and upcoming ceasefire deadlines, the spotlight remains on whether large-cap cryptos can sustain their momentum independent of broader equ...

articles.title: Dollar Recovers on Rising Tensions Over the Strait of Hormuz
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Wednesday rose to a 1-week high and finished up by +0.20%. The dollar found support on Wednesday as the escalation of the US-Iran war boosted safe-haven demand for the dollar. Iran seized two ships on Wednesday in the Strait of Hormuz for "endangering maritime...

articles.title: NZD/USD consolidates gains as hawkish RBNZ outlook meets cautious risk sentiment
articles.expand: NZD/USD holds firm on Wednesday, with the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) outperforming its major peers on the back of a hawkish Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) outlook.

articles.title: Markets steady with ceasefire talks in the balance, Warsh confirmation hearing
articles.expand: In today's episode, Nick and Jonny discuss a relative calm in the markets as US-Iran ceasefire negotiations hang in the balance, as well as dissecting Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearing and potential implications for US monetary policy.

articles.title: Iran parliamentary speaker Ghalibaf: Complete ceasefire only makes sense without blockade
articles.expand: It's important to note that with all the MB Ghalibaf posts, that his twitter account appears to be run from Los Angeles by someone connected with him, not the man himself. That's led to some bizarre posts about trading brent crude and it also calls into question a message like this that might be more interpretation than a finely-crafted message. An...

articles.title: EUR/USD slips as Hormuz tensions persist despite Iran ceasefire extension
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) weakens against the US Dollar (USD) on Wednesday, as ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz offset the impact of the US-Iran ceasefire extension, keeping the Greenback supported.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD steadies as Iran deadlock keeps Dollar bid in check
articles.expand: GBP/USD holds steady on Wednesday as geopolitical tensions remain high amid the lack of progress toward resuming negotiations between the US and Iran. An absent economic schedule in the US, keeps traders leaning on the latest UK inflation figures, which showed the effects of the energy shock.

articles.title: AUD/USD climbs on extended US-Iran ceasefire, firm RBA rate hike expectations
articles.expand: AUD/USD trades in positive territory on Wednesday, around 0.7160 at the time of writing, up 0.12% on the day.

articles.title: USD/JPY steadies as Middle East tensions offset softer yields
articles.expand: The USD/JPY climbed near the 159.30 price region on Wednesday, consolidating around recent highs as markets digest fresh developments in the Middle East and shift monetary policy expectations.

articles.title: GBP/USD steadies as Iran deadlock keeps US Dollar bid in check
articles.expand: GBP/USD holds steady on Wednesday as geopolitical tensions remain high amid the lack of progress toward resuming negotiations between the US and Iran. An absent economic schedule in the US keeps traders leaning on the latest UK inflation figures, which showed the effects of the energy shock.

articles.title: ECB: Growth risks temper hike urgency – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale’s Anatoli Annenkov expects the European Central Bank (ECB) to keep rates unchanged next week as focus shifts toward Euro Area growth and medium-term core inflation.

articles.title: EUR/GBP weakens as UK CPI lifts GBP; downside seen below 200-day SMA
articles.expand: EUR/GBP trades on the back foot on Wednesday as UK inflation data lifts the British Pound (GBP), pressuring the Euro (EUR), with the cross extending losses for the second consecutive day. At the time of writing, EUR/GBP is trading around 0.8680, its lowest level since March 31.

articles.title: Eurozone: Stagflation risk builds after Iran conflict – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank strategists argue that the Iran war delivers a stagflationary shock to the Eurozone, lifting inflation and depressing Gross Domestic Product (GDP).

articles.title: UK inflation: Mixed CPI keeps BoE cautious – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities notes UK headline CPI matched expectations at 3.3% year-on-year in March, reflecting early post-conflict price effects, especially from motor fuels.

articles.title: Reuters poll: Economists see Fed holding rates to September before cautious rate cuts
articles.expand: The latest Reuters surveys of economists suggest a notable shift in expectations regarding the trajectory of US monetary policy, with the timing of potential easing likely to come later than previously anticipated.

articles.title: ECB: Limited case for renewed rate hikes – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Dr. Vincent Stamer compares its Euro area inflation projections with European Central Bank (ECB) staff forecasts and concludes they are broadly aligned under the mild Iran War scenario.

articles.title: Euro area: Energy shock seen more disinflationary – Nomura
articles.expand: Nomura economists argue that the latest energy price surge will be a larger drag on Euro area growth than a persistent inflation shock. They stress weaker labour markets in Northern Europe, limited fiscal space, more spare capacity and slowing wage growth.

articles.title: Oil: Supply shock revives stagflation worries – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong highlight that Brent is nearing USD100 as flows through the Strait of Hormuz are unlikely to resume early, pushing markets into an Oil–stagflation channel.

articles.expand: EUR/USD is starting to tilt bearish as rising risk aversion and renewed dollar demand pressure the pair toward the 1.1700–1.1660 support zone.

articles.title: Jordan reports fall in tourist income as war impact bites
articles.expand: Jordan’s tourism income, a major hard-currency earner, receded in the first quarter of 2026 as the kingdom suffered from the spillovers of war between Iran and the US-Israeli alliance. Like other countries in the oil-rich Gulf, Jordan came under repeated Iranian drone and missile attacks during the conflict. The nation’s power sector also suffered ...

articles.title: UK inflation hits 3.3% as Iran war drives energy costs higher
articles.expand: British consumer prices rose at a faster pace in March, reaching 3.3% as the ongoing Iran war sent fuel prices soaring. The data has forced markets to reassess expectations for the Bank of England, with a previously anticipated interest rate cut next week now looking very unlikely.

articles.title: Iran war: Jet fuel crisis deepens as Lufthansa cuts flights
articles.expand: The impact of jet fuel shortages because of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is starting to burn. Lufthansa has canceled 20,000 flights and experts warn more cancellations and price hikes are on the way.

While inflationary pressures remain a concern for policymakers, they are prioritizing stability amid the significant geopolitical and economic uncertainty.
